input.new_string:
### Phase 1 — Identity + conversation persistence (highest value, biggest surface)

**Goal:** Learners land on the app, pick themselves, and can pick up where they left off. SAGE "remembers" them across refreshes and redeploys.

**1a. Profile identity**
- **Add `list_user_profiles()` tool** in [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) — returns `[{"id": ..., "name": ..., "level": ..., "lastActiveAt": ...}]` by scanning `data/users/*.json`. Reuse the existing path-traversal guard pattern from `save_user_profile`.
- **Learner picker in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — on first load (no `st.session_state.learner`), render a centered `st.selectbox` of profiles + a "New learner" option (prompts for a name, then calls `save_user_profile` with a minimal template derived from the `novice` section of [user-profile.schema.json](data/schemas/user-profile.schema.json)).
- Selection loads the JSON into `st.session_state.learner` and unlocks the chat.

**1b. Conversation persistence (the "pick up where they left off" piece)**
- **New module [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py)** — single-responsibility module with four pure functions:
  - `new_session(user_id) -> session_id` — mints a UUID, creates `data/interactions/<user_id>/<session_id>.json` using the existing [interaction-log.schema.json](data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json).
  - `append_turn(user_id, session_id, role, content, metadata=None)` — atomic append to the log's `turns` array; updates `lastActiveAt` on the profile. Path-traversal guarded.
  - `load_session(user_id, session_id) -> list[turn]` — reads a log and returns messages in Anthropic-API-shape (`{"role": "user"|"assistant", "content": "..."}`).
  - `latest_session(user_id) -> session_id | None` — scans the learner's interactions dir, returns most recent by `timestamp` field, or `None` if no logs.
- **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — after picker, if `latest_session(user_id)` exists and has ≥2 turns, show two buttons: **"Resume last session"** (loads turns into `st.session_state.messages`, keeps the existing `session_id`) and **"Start fresh"** (calls `new_session`). If no prior log, skip the prompt and start fresh silently.
- **Thread-cap for API calls** — when passing `messages` to `client.beta.messages.tool_runner`, cap at the last 40 turns. (Deferred enhancement: summarize older turns — out of scope for v2.2.)
- **Wire writes into the main loop** — in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py), call `append_turn` right after each user message and right after each assistant response. Keep `st.session_state.messages` as the in-memory mirror so the UI stays responsive.
- **"Start new session" button** in sidebar — clears `st.session_state.messages`, mints a fresh `session_id`, keeps the learner.

**1c. Identity on screen**
- **Header strip** — above `st.chat_input`, render `st.caption` with `👋 {name} · {level} · Today's focus: {focus_dimension}`. `focus_dimension` = lowest-scoring of the 5 assessment dimensions (deterministic, computed from `dimensionScores`). Labeled as "focus" (prescriptive) not "weakest area" (descriptive).
- **SAGE awareness of learner** — inject a small context block into the system prompt at runtime in [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py): `LEARNER_CONTEXT: name={name}, level={level}, dimensionScores={...}`. Lets skills address the learner by name and adapt vocabulary to their level without a tool round-trip.

**1d. Infrastructure**
- **Railway persistent volume** — configure via Railway dashboard: mount a volume at `/app/data`. On container boot, if the volume is empty, copy the seed profiles from the image into it. Document the volume setup in [README.md](README.md) under "Railway deployment."
- **CHANGELOG.md entry** — per project convention, append a dated entry describing Phase 1 additions.

- **"Start new session" button** in sidebar — clears `st.session_state.messages`, mints a fresh `session_id`, keeps the learner.

**1c. Identity on screen**
- **Header strip** — above `st.chat_input`, render `st.caption` with `👋 {name} · {level} · Today's focus: {focus_dimension}`. `focus_dimension` = lowest-scoring of the 5 assessment dimensions (deterministic, computed from `dimensionScores`). Labeled as "focus" (prescriptive) not "weakest area" (descriptive).
- **SAGE awareness of learner** — inject a small context block into the system prompt at runtime in [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py): `LEARNER_CONTEXT: name={name}, level={level}, dimensionScores={...}`. Lets skills address the learner by name and adapt vocabulary to their level without a tool round-trip.

**1d. Infrastructure**
- **Railway persistent volume** — configure via Railway dashboard: mount a volume at `/app/data`. On container boot, if the volume is empty, copy the seed profiles from the image into it. Document the volume setup in [README.md](README.md) under "Railway deployment."
- **CHANGELOG.md entry** — per project convention, append a dated entry describing Phase 1 additions.
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# SAGE on Railway — Progress Visibility + Personalization

## Context

SAGE's Railway deploy was just unblocked (commit [08ae1f4](https://github.com/)) — it now runs as a Streamlit chat app on a public URL with Claude Opus 4.6 and 18 pedagogical skills. Pedagogy is complete; the UX surface is the weak spot:

- **No learner identity** — every visit is anonymous; profiles in `data/users/*.json` are never loaded. `sage/app.py` just initializes `st.session_state.messages = []` and never touches the profile tools.
- **No conversation continuity** — the chat thread lives only in `st.session_state.messages` (in-memory). A refresh, redeploy, or tab close wipes it. There's a `data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json` designed for session transcripts, but nothing writes to it.
- **No progress visibility** — the 5-dimension × 4-competency model SAGE tracks internally (schema already there) is invisible to the learner, so the "see yourself grow" pedagogy has no external surface.
- **Railway filesystem is ephemeral** — without a mounted volume, every write (profile or transcript) is lost on redeploy.

The cohort is about to demo SAGE. We want learners to feel **known** (their profile loads, their level is visible) and **tracked** (they can see what they've practiced and what's next) — without tripping the OLM/ed-tech pitfall of ambient dashboards that demotivate low performers (Bodily & Verbert 2018).

The research-backed UX shape (see brainstorm in this session): **always-visible minimal header + collapsible detail drawer + inline end-of-session recap card**. Borrowed from Khanmigo's session summary, Claude.ai's collapsible rail, Duolingo's jewel-level badge. Avoids tabs, XP, streaks, and ambient raw scores.

## Design Overview

Four user-facing additions to [sage/app.py](sage/app.py):

1. **Learner picker** on app start — no auth, trust-based, cohort-friendly. Dropdown of existing `data/users/*.json` profiles + "New learner" option that prompts for a name.
2. **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — if the picked learner has a recent session log, SAGE greets with "Welcome back. Last time we worked on output evaluation — want to pick up where you left off, or start fresh?"
3. **Header strip** (always visible, top of chat column) — `👋 cyril · Practitioner · Today's focus: Output Evaluation`. One line, no numbers. Prescriptive, not graded.
4. **"📊 My Progress" expander** in the sidebar — collapsed by default. Opens to 5-dimension radar + 4 competency bars + reflection log (read-only).
5. **Inline session-recap card** — renders as a chat-stream message after the closing reflection. "You practiced *Output Evaluation*. Ethical Reasoning +0.5. Next: try a prompt-crafting scenario?"

Plus two pieces of infrastructure:
- **Session store** — a new `sage/session_store.py` that persists the chat thread to `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` using the existing `interaction-log.schema.json`. Profile JSONs stay clean (identity + scores only); transcripts live in their own tree.
- **Railway persistent volume** mounted at `/app/data` so both `data/users/*.json` and `data/interactions/**` survive redeploys.

## Phased Rollout

### Phase 1 — Personalization foundation (highest value, smallest surface)

**Goal:** Learners land on the app, pick themselves, and see their identity + level.

- **Add `list_user_profiles()` tool** in [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) — returns `[{"name": "...", "level": "...", "display_name": "..."}]` by scanning `data/users/*.json`. Reuses the existing path-traversal guard pattern from `save_user_profile`.
- **Learner picker in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — on first load (no `st.session_state.learner`), render a centered `st.selectbox` of profiles + a "New learner" option. Selection loads the JSON into `st.session_state.learner` and the chat starts with a warm `onboarding` or "welcome back" system hint.
- **Header strip** — above `st.chat_input`, render `st.caption` with `👋 {name} · {level} · Today's focus: {focus_dimension}`. `focus_dimension` = lowest-scoring of the 5 assessment dimensions (deterministic, fast). Labeled as "focus" (prescriptive) not "weakest area" (descriptive).
- **Railway persistent volume** — configure via Railway dashboard: mount a volume at `/app/data`. Existing seed profiles copy on first boot if empty. Document in [README.md](README.md) under deployment.
- **CHANGELOG.md entry** — per project convention (see memory feedback), append a dated entry describing Phase 1 additions.

### Phase 2 — Progress drawer

**Goal:** Learners who want detail can open the drawer and see where they stand and where they've been.

- **New helper module [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py)** with pure functions:
  - `render_level_badge(profile)` — styled label, used by header strip too.
  - `render_dimension_radar(profile)` — Plotly radar chart over the 5 dimensions (Streamlit has native Plotly support, no extra deps beyond what `pyproject.toml` already allows).
  - `render_competency_bars(profile)` — 4 horizontal bars, each with a one-sentence gloss next to the score (avoids the "high inference cost" OLM pitfall).
  - `render_reflection_log(profile)` — most recent 5 reflections from `profile["history"]`, collapsed further behind a "Show more" toggle.
- **Sidebar wiring in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — `with st.sidebar.expander("📊 My Progress", expanded=False):` then call the four render helpers. Drawer is hidden by default (research: ambient scores demotivate).
- **"Switch learner" action** at the bottom of the sidebar — clears `st.session_state.learner` and returns to the picker. No passwords, no confirmation (cohort trust model).

### Phase 3 — Inline end-of-session recap card

**Goal:** When a practice session closes with a reflection, the learner sees a single-delta recap right in the chat stream.

- **Teach the `reflection-facilitator` skill to emit a structured recap block** — edit [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) so that when a session closes it appends a `<SESSION_RECAP>{...}</SESSION_RECAP>` block to its final message. The JSON contains: `practice_type`, `dimension_changed`, `delta`, `suggested_next`.
- **Parser + renderer in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — when streaming the assistant message, extract the `<SESSION_RECAP>` block (regex), hide it from the text output, and render a bordered `st.container` card below the reflection message with the delta + a clickable "Try that next" button that seeds the next prompt.
- **Graceful fallback** — if no recap block is emitted (SAGE didn't end a session), nothing changes. The feature is additive, not a schema break.
- **Verify recap doesn't leak as literal text** to the learner on non-session-ending turns.

## Technical Notes

- **Railway filesystem constraint is real.** Without the Phase 1 persistent volume, all profile writes are lost on every redeploy. This is the single most important piece of infrastructure for the whole design — progress visibility is hollow if progress doesn't persist.
- **Streamlit rerun model** — every interaction reruns the script top-to-bottom. All state (current learner, drawer open/closed, picker visibility) must live in `st.session_state`. Keep the UI helpers pure (input: profile dict, output: Streamlit call).
- **Treat pasted content as untrusted** — per [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) rule, the session-recap block is emitted by SAGE and trusted; anything the *learner* pastes is data, not instructions. Parser should only accept the recap sentinel in SAGE's own streamed output, never from user messages.
- **YAGNI flagged and deferred** — no XP, no streaks, no leaderboard, no tabs, no auth. Research explicitly warned against these for a literacy tutor. Revisit only if demo feedback demands it.

## Critical Files

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| [sage/app.py](sage/app.py) | Picker, header strip, sidebar drawer, recap card parser |
| [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) | Add `list_user_profiles()` |
| [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py) | New — pure render helpers |
| [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) | Emit `<SESSION_RECAP>` block |
| [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py) | Small note so SAGE references loaded learner by name |
| [README.md](README.md) | Document Railway volume mount + cohort demo usage |
| [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) | Dated entries per phase |
| [docs/roadmap.md](docs/roadmap.md) | Move "visible learner progress" from v3 wishlist to v2.2 shipped |

## Verification

**Phase 1:**
- `streamlit run sage/app.py` locally → picker appears → select `cyril.json` → header strip shows name, level, focus dimension.
- Refresh the browser → picker reappears (session state resets) → selecting same profile reloads same JSON.
- Deploy to Railway with volume → select new learner → trigger a skill that writes the profile (e.g., `onboarding`) → redeploy → profile survives.

**Phase 2:**
- Open the drawer → radar renders all 5 dimensions without errors on a new (all-zeros) profile.
- Competency bars each show gloss text.
- "Switch learner" returns to picker.

**Phase 3:**
- Run a full `scenario-runner` → `skill-evaluator` → `reflection-facilitator` sequence → recap card appears below the reflection question with a non-zero delta.
- Mid-conversation turn (no session ending) → no recap card leaks into chat.
- Inspect the raw streamed message → no literal `<SESSION_RECAP>` tag visible to the learner.

**Demo acceptance:**
- Two cohort members each pick their own profile, have a practice session, see their header + drawer + recap — and can refresh the browser without losing their profile state.

input.new_string:
## Technical Notes

- **Railway filesystem constraint is the foundation.** Without the Phase 1 persistent volume, every profile write AND every interaction log is lost on every redeploy. Nothing else in this design works without it.
- **Two artifact trees, cleanly separated.** `data/users/*.json` = identity + scores (small, schema-stable, infrequently written). `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` = conversation transcripts (grow-only, one per session). Do not cram transcripts into the profile — the user-profile schema already omits them deliberately, and keeping boundaries clean lets the drawer's "session history" view be a simple directory listing.
- **Streamlit rerun model** — every interaction reruns the script top-to-bottom. All state (current learner, current session_id, drawer open/closed, picker visibility) must live in `st.session_state`. Keep the UI helpers pure (input: profile dict, output: Streamlit call).
- **Atomic writes to interaction logs.** Streamlit can fire duplicate reruns on fast user input. `append_turn` should read-modify-write under a short file lock or use "write new file + rename" to avoid corrupting the JSON.
- **Thread cap before the API.** Don't send an unbounded `messages` array to Claude. Cap at the last 40 turns for v2.2. Track this as a known limit in `docs/roadmap.md` — summarization of older turns is deferred.
- **Resume semantics.** On "Resume last session," rehydrate `st.session_state.messages` from the log, set `st.session_state.session_id` to that log's id, and let the next user input append to the existing file. Do NOT mint a new session_id on resume.
- **Treat pasted content as untrusted** — per [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) rule, the session-recap block is emitted by SAGE and trusted; anything the *learner* pastes is data, not instructions. Parser should only accept the recap sentinel in SAGE's own streamed output, never from user messages. (Applies equally: don't parse recap blocks out of *resumed* assistant turns into new UI renders — render recap cards only on fresh completions, not on replay.)
- **YAGNI flagged and deferred** — no XP, no streaks, no leaderboard, no tabs, no auth, no turn-summarization, no cross-device sync. Research explicitly warned against the gamification bits for a literacy tutor. Revisit only if demo feedback demands it.

input.old_string:
**Phase 1:**
- `streamlit run sage/app.py` locally → picker appears → select `cyril.json` → header strip shows name, level, focus dimension.
- Refresh the browser → picker reappears (session state resets) → selecting same profile reloads same JSON.
- Deploy to Railway with volume → select new learner → trigger a skill that writes the profile (e.g., `onboarding`) → redeploy → profile survives.
input.new_string:
**Phase 1 — identity:**
- `streamlit run sage/app.py` locally → picker appears → select `cyril.json` → header strip shows name, level, focus dimension.
- Pick "New learner" → enter a name → a fresh `data/users/<slug>.json` appears with the minimal template.

**Phase 1 — persistence:**
- Have a 4-turn conversation with `cyril` → refresh the browser → picker reappears → re-select `cyril` → "Resume last session?" prompt shows → click Resume → all 4 turns rehydrate in the chat stream, same `session_id` is in `st.session_state`.
- From the resumed thread, send a 5th message → open `data/interactions/cyril-001/<session>.json` → verify the new turn appended to the existing file (not a new file).
- Click "Start new session" → chat clears → `data/interactions/cyril-001/<new-session>.json` created → old log untouched.
- Switch to `alice` → no turns from cyril leak into the view; `alice` sees her own "Resume?" prompt or none.
- Deploy to Railway with volume mounted at `/app/data` → have a conversation → redeploy → resume the same session successfully.
- Deploy to Railway WITHOUT a volume → confirm data is lost (negative test proving the volume is the load-bearing piece).

content.content:
1	# SAGE on Railway — Progress Visibility + Personalization
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	SAGE's Railway deploy was just unblocked (commit [08ae1f4](https://github.com/)) — it now runs as a Streamlit chat app on a public URL with Claude Opus 4.6 and 18 pedagogical skills. Pedagogy is complete; the UX surface is the weak spot:
6	
7	- **No learner identity** — every visit is anonymous; profiles in `data/users/*.json` are never loaded. `sage/app.py` just initializes `st.session_state.messages = []` and never touches the profile tools.
8	- **No conversation continuity** — the chat thread lives only in `st.session_state.messages` (in-memory). A refresh, redeploy, or tab close wipes it. There's a `data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json` designed for session transcripts, but nothing writes to it.
9	- **No progress visibility** — the 5-dimension × 4-competency model SAGE tracks internally (schema already there) is invisible to the learner, so the "see yourself grow" pedagogy has no external surface.
10	- **Railway filesystem is ephemeral** — without a mounted volume, every write (profile or transcript) is lost on redeploy.
11	
12	The cohort is about to demo SAGE. We want learners to feel **known** (their profile loads, their level is visible) and **tracked** (they can see what they've practiced and what's next) — without tripping the OLM/ed-tech pitfall of ambient dashboards that demotivate low performers (Bodily & Verbert 2018).
13	
14	The research-backed UX shape (see brainstorm in this session): **always-visible minimal header + collapsible detail drawer + inline end-of-session recap card**. Borrowed from Khanmigo's session summary, Claude.ai's collapsible rail, Duolingo's jewel-level badge. Avoids tabs, XP, streaks, and ambient raw scores.
15	
16	## Design Overview
17	
18	Four user-facing additions to [sage/app.py](sage/app.py):
19	
20	1. **Learner picker** on app start — no auth, trust-based, cohort-friendly. Dropdown of existing `data/users/*.json` profiles + "New learner" option that prompts for a name.
21	2. **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — if the picked learner has a recent session log, SAGE greets with "Welcome back. Last time we worked on output evaluation — want to pick up where you left off, or start fresh?"
22	3. **Header strip** (always visible, top of chat column) — `👋 cyril · Practitioner · Today's focus: Output Evaluation`. One line, no numbers. Prescriptive, not graded.
23	4. **"📊 My Progress" expander** in the sidebar — collapsed by default. Opens to 5-dimension radar + 4 competency bars + reflection log (read-only).
24	5. **Inline session-recap card** — renders as a chat-stream message after the closing reflection. "You practiced *Output Evaluation*. Ethical Reasoning +0.5. Next: try a prompt-crafting scenario?"
25	
26	Plus two pieces of infrastructure:
27	- **Session store** — a new `sage/session_store.py` that persists the chat thread to `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` using the existing `interaction-log.schema.json`. Profile JSONs stay clean (identity + scores only); transcripts live in their own tree.
28	- **Railway persistent volume** mounted at `/app/data` so both `data/users/*.json` and `data/interactions/**` survive redeploys.
29	
30	## Phased Rollout
31	
32	### Phase 1 — Identity + conversation persistence (highest value, biggest surface)
33	
34	**Goal:** Learners land on the app, pick themselves, and can pick up where they left off. SAGE "remembers" them across refreshes and redeploys.
35	
36	**1a. Profile identity**
37	- **Add `list_user_profiles()` tool** in [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) — returns `[{"id": ..., "name": ..., "level": ..., "lastActiveAt": ...}]` by scanning `data/users/*.json`. Reuse the existing path-traversal guard pattern from `save_user_profile`.
38	- **Learner picker in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — on first load (no `st.session_state.learner`), render a centered `st.selectbox` of profiles + a "New learner" option (prompts for a name, then calls `save_user_profile` with a minimal template derived from the `novice` section of [user-profile.schema.json](data/schemas/user-profile.schema.json)).
39	- Selection loads the JSON into `st.session_state.learner` and unlocks the chat.
40	
41	**1b. Conversation persistence (the "pick up where they left off" piece)**
42	- **New module [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py)** — single-responsibility module with four pure functions:
43	  - `new_session(user_id) -> session_id` — mints a UUID, creates `data/interactions/<user_id>/<session_id>.json` using the existing [interaction-log.schema.json](data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json).
44	  - `append_turn(user_id, session_id, role, content, metadata=None)` — atomic append to the log's `turns` array; updates `lastActiveAt` on the profile. Path-traversal guarded.
45	  - `load_session(user_id, session_id) -> list[turn]` — reads a log and returns messages in Anthropic-API-shape (`{"role": "user"|"assistant", "content": "..."}`).
46	  - `latest_session(user_id) -> session_id | None` — scans the learner's interactions dir, returns most recent by `timestamp` field, or `None` if no logs.
47	- **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — after picker, if `latest_session(user_id)` exists and has ≥2 turns, show two buttons: **"Resume last session"** (loads turns into `st.session_state.messages`, keeps the existing `session_id`) and **"Start fresh"** (calls `new_session`). If no prior log, skip the prompt and start fresh silently.
48	- **Thread-cap for API calls** — when passing `messages` to `client.beta.messages.tool_runner`, cap at the last 40 turns. (Deferred enhancement: summarize older turns — out of scope for v2.2.)
49	- **Wire writes into the main loop** — in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py), call `append_turn` right after each user message and right after each assistant response. Keep `st.session_state.messages` as the in-memory mirror so the UI stays responsive.
50	- **"Start new session" button** in sidebar — clears `st.session_state.messages`, mints a fresh `session_id`, keeps the learner.
51	
52	**1c. Identity on screen**
53	- **Header strip** — above `st.chat_input`, render `st.caption` with `👋 {name} · {level} · Today's focus: {focus_dimension}`. `focus_dimension` = lowest-scoring of the 5 assessment dimensions (deterministic, computed from `dimensionScores`). Labeled as "focus" (prescriptive) not "weakest area" (descriptive).
54	- **SAGE awareness of learner** — inject a small context block into the system prompt at runtime in [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py): `LEARNER_CONTEXT: name={name}, level={level}, dimensionScores={...}`. Lets skills address the learner by name and adapt vocabulary to their level without a tool round-trip.
55	
56	**1d. Infrastructure**
57	- **Railway persistent volume** — configure via Railway dashboard: mount a volume at `/app/data`. On container boot, if the volume is empty, copy the seed profiles from the image into it. Document the volume setup in [README.md](README.md) under "Railway deployment."
58	- **CHANGELOG.md entry** — per project convention, append a dated entry describing Phase 1 additions.
59	
60	### Phase 2 — Progress drawer
61	
62	**Goal:** Learners who want detail can open the drawer and see where they stand and where they've been.
63	
64	- **New helper module [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py)** with pure functions:
65	  - `render_level_badge(profile)` — styled label, used by header strip too.
66	  - `render_dimension_radar(profile)` — Plotly radar chart over the 5 dimensions (Streamlit has native Plotly support, no extra deps beyond what `pyproject.toml` already allows).
67	  - `render_competency_bars(profile)` — 4 horizontal bars, each with a one-sentence gloss next to the score (avoids the "high inference cost" OLM pitfall).
68	  - `render_reflection_log(profile)` — most recent 5 reflections from `profile["history"]`, collapsed further behind a "Show more" toggle.
69	- **Sidebar wiring in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — `with st.sidebar.expander("📊 My Progress", expanded=False):` then call the four render helpers. Drawer is hidden by default (research: ambient scores demotivate).
70	- **"Switch learner" action** at the bottom of the sidebar — clears `st.session_state.learner` and returns to the picker. No passwords, no confirmation (cohort trust model).
71	
72	### Phase 3 — Inline end-of-session recap card
73	
74	**Goal:** When a practice session closes with a reflection, the learner sees a single-delta recap right in the chat stream.
75	
76	- **Teach the `reflection-facilitator` skill to emit a structured recap block** — edit [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) so that when a session closes it appends a `<SESSION_RECAP>{...}</SESSION_RECAP>` block to its final message. The JSON contains: `practice_type`, `dimension_changed`, `delta`, `suggested_next`.
77	- **Parser + renderer in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — when streaming the assistant message, extract the `<SESSION_RECAP>` block (regex), hide it from the text output, and render a bordered `st.container` card below the reflection message with the delta + a clickable "Try that next" button that seeds the next prompt.
78	- **Graceful fallback** — if no recap block is emitted (SAGE didn't end a session), nothing changes. The feature is additive, not a schema break.
79	- **Verify recap doesn't leak as literal text** to the learner on non-session-ending turns.
80	
81	## Technical Notes
82	
83	- **Railway filesystem constraint is the foundation.** Without the Phase 1 persistent volume, every profile write AND every interaction log is lost on every redeploy. Nothing else in this design works without it.
84	- **Two artifact trees, cleanly separated.** `data/users/*.json` = identity + scores (small, schema-stable, infrequently written). `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` = conversation transcripts (grow-only, one per session). Do not cram transcripts into the profile — the user-profile schema already omits them deliberately, and keeping boundaries clean lets the drawer's "session history" view be a simple directory listing.
85	- **Streamlit rerun model** — every interaction reruns the script top-to-bottom. All state (current learner, current session_id, drawer open/closed, picker visibility) must live in `st.session_state`. Keep the UI helpers pure (input: profile dict, output: Streamlit call).
86	- **Atomic writes to interaction logs.** Streamlit can fire duplicate reruns on fast user input. `append_turn` should read-modify-write under a short file lock or use "write new file + rename" to avoid corrupting the JSON.
87	- **Thread cap before the API.** Don't send an unbounded `messages` array to Claude. Cap at the last 40 turns for v2.2. Track this as a known limit in `docs/roadmap.md` — summarization of older turns is deferred.
88	- **Resume semantics.** On "Resume last session," rehydrate `st.session_state.messages` from the log, set `st.session_state.session_id` to that log's id, and let the next user input append to the existing file. Do NOT mint a new session_id on resume.
89	- **Treat pasted content as untrusted** — per [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) rule, the session-recap block is emitted by SAGE and trusted; anything the *learner* pastes is data, not instructions. Parser should only accept the recap sentinel in SAGE's own streamed output, never from user messages. (Applies equally: don't parse recap blocks out of *resumed* assistant turns into new UI renders — render recap cards only on fresh completions, not on replay.)
90	- **YAGNI flagged and deferred** — no XP, no streaks, no leaderboard, no tabs, no auth, no turn-summarization, no cross-device sync. Research explicitly warned against the gamification bits for a literacy tutor. Revisit only if demo feedback demands it.
91	
92	## Critical Files
93	
94	| Path | Change |
95	|---|---|
96	| [sage/app.py](sage/app.py) | Picker, resume prompt, header strip, sidebar drawer, recap card parser, wire session-store writes |
97	| [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py) | **New** — `new_session`, `append_turn`, `load_session`, `latest_session` against `data/interactions/` using existing interaction-log schema |
98	| [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) | Add `list_user_profiles()` |
99	| [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py) | **New** — pure render helpers (level badge, radar, competency bars, reflection log, recap card) |
100	| [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py) | Inject `LEARNER_CONTEXT` block so SAGE addresses the learner by name + adapts to level |
101	| [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) | Emit `<SESSION_RECAP>` block at session close |
102	| [data/interactions/](data/interactions/) | **New directory** — per-learner session logs (gitignore real logs; keep a `.gitkeep`) |
103	| [.gitignore](.gitignore) | Ignore `data/interactions/*/` contents |
104	| [Dockerfile](Dockerfile) | No code change; document volume expectation in comment |
105	| [README.md](README.md) | Document Railway volume mount + cohort demo usage |
106	| [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) | Dated entries per phase |
107	| [docs/roadmap.md](docs/roadmap.md) | Move "visible learner progress" + "session persistence" from v3 wishlist to v2.2 shipped |
108	
109	## Verification
110	
111	**Phase 1 — identity:**
112	- `streamlit run sage/app.py` locally → picker appears → select `cyril.json` → header strip shows name, level, focus dimension.
113	- Pick "New learner" → enter a name → a fresh `data/users/<slug>.json` appears with the minimal template.
114	
115	**Phase 1 — persistence:**
116	- Have a 4-turn conversation with `cyril` → refresh the browser → picker reappears → re-select `cyril` → "Resume last session?" prompt shows → click Resume → all 4 turns rehydrate in the chat stream, same `session_id` is in `st.session_state`.
117	- From the resumed thread, send a 5th message → open `data/interactions/cyril-001/<session>.json` → verify the new turn appended to the existing file (not a new file).
118	- Click "Start new session" → chat clears → `data/interactions/cyril-001/<new-session>.json` created → old log untouched.
119	- Switch to `alice` → no turns from cyril leak into the view; `alice` sees her own "Resume?" prompt or none.
120	- Deploy to Railway with volume mounted at `/app/data` → have a conversation → redeploy → resume the same session successfully.
121	- Deploy to Railway WITHOUT a volume → confirm data is lost (negative test proving the volume is the load-bearing piece).
122	
123	**Phase 2:**
124	- Open the drawer → radar renders all 5 dimensions without errors on a new (all-zeros) profile.
125	- Competency bars each show gloss text.
126	- "Switch learner" returns to picker.
127	
128	**Phase 3:**
129	- Run a full `scenario-runner` → `skill-evaluator` → `reflection-facilitator` sequence → recap card appears below the reflection question with a non-zero delta.
130	- Mid-conversation turn (no session ending) → no recap card leaks into chat.
131	- Inspect the raw streamed message → no literal `<SESSION_RECAP>` tag visible to the learner.
132	
133	**Demo acceptance:**
134	- Two cohort members each pick their own profile, have a practice session, see their header + drawer + recap — and can refresh the browser without losing their profile state.
135

# SAGE on Railway — Progress Visibility + Personalization

## Context

SAGE's Railway deploy was just unblocked (commit [08ae1f4](https://github.com/)) — it now runs as a Streamlit chat app on a public URL with Claude Opus 4.6 and 18 pedagogical skills. Pedagogy is complete; the UX surface is the weak spot:

- **No learner identity** — every visit is anonymous; profiles in `data/users/*.json` are never loaded. `sage/app.py` just initializes `st.session_state.messages = []` and never touches the profile tools.
- **No conversation continuity** — the chat thread lives only in `st.session_state.messages` (in-memory). A refresh, redeploy, or tab close wipes it. There's a `data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json` designed for session transcripts, but nothing writes to it.
- **No progress visibility** — the 5-dimension × 4-competency model SAGE tracks internally (schema already there) is invisible to the learner, so the "see yourself grow" pedagogy has no external surface.
- **Railway filesystem is ephemeral** — without a mounted volume, every write (profile or transcript) is lost on redeploy.

The cohort is about to demo SAGE. We want learners to feel **known** (their profile loads, their level is visible) and **tracked** (they can see what they've practiced and what's next) — without tripping the OLM/ed-tech pitfall of ambient dashboards that demotivate low performers (Bodily & Verbert 2018).

The research-backed UX shape (see brainstorm in this session): **always-visible minimal header + collapsible detail drawer + inline end-of-session recap card**. Borrowed from Khanmigo's session summary, Claude.ai's collapsible rail, Duolingo's jewel-level badge. Avoids tabs, XP, streaks, and ambient raw scores.

## Design Overview

Four user-facing additions to [sage/app.py](sage/app.py):

1. **Learner picker** on app start — no auth, trust-based, cohort-friendly. Dropdown of existing `data/users/*.json` profiles + "New learner" option that prompts for a name.
2. **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — if the picked learner has a recent session log, SAGE greets with "Welcome back. Last time we worked on output evaluation — want to pick up where you left off, or start fresh?"
3. **Header strip** (always visible, top of chat column) — `👋 cyril · Practitioner · Today's focus: Output Evaluation`. One line, no numbers. Prescriptive, not graded.
4. **"📊 My Progress" expander** in the sidebar — collapsed by default. Opens to 5-dimension radar + 4 competency bars + reflection log (read-only).
5. **Inline session-recap card** — renders as a chat-stream message after the closing reflection. "You practiced *Output Evaluation*. Ethical Reasoning +0.5. Next: try a prompt-crafting scenario?"

Plus two pieces of infrastructure:
- **Session store** — a new `sage/session_store.py` that persists the chat thread to `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` using the existing `interaction-log.schema.json`. Profile JSONs stay clean (identity + scores only); transcripts live in their own tree.
- **Railway persistent volume** mounted at `/app/data` so both `data/users/*.json` and `data/interactions/**` survive redeploys.

## Phased Rollout

### Phase 1 — Identity + conversation persistence (highest value, biggest surface)

**Goal:** Learners land on the app, pick themselves, and can pick up where they left off. SAGE "remembers" them across refreshes and redeploys.

**1a. Profile identity**
- **Add `list_user_profiles()` tool** in [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) — returns `[{"id": ..., "name": ..., "level": ..., "lastActiveAt": ...}]` by scanning `data/users/*.json`. Reuse the existing path-traversal guard pattern from `save_user_profile`.
- **Learner picker in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — on first load (no `st.session_state.learner`), render a centered `st.selectbox` of profiles + a "New learner" option (prompts for a name, then calls `save_user_profile` with a minimal template derived from the `novice` section of [user-profile.schema.json](data/schemas/user-profile.schema.json)).
- Selection loads the JSON into `st.session_state.learner` and unlocks the chat.

**1b. Conversation persistence (the "pick up where they left off" piece)**
- **New module [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py)** — single-responsibility module with four pure functions:
  - `new_session(user_id) -> session_id` — mints a UUID, creates `data/interactions/<user_id>/<session_id>.json` using the existing [interaction-log.schema.json](data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json).
  - `append_turn(user_id, session_id, role, content, metadata=None)` — atomic append to the log's `turns` array; updates `lastActiveAt` on the profile. Path-traversal guarded.
  - `load_session(user_id, session_id) -> list[turn]` — reads a log and returns messages in Anthropic-API-shape (`{"role": "user"|"assistant", "content": "..."}`).
  - `latest_session(user_id) -> session_id | None` — scans the learner's interactions dir, returns most recent by `timestamp` field, or `None` if no logs.
- **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — after picker, if `latest_session(user_id)` exists and has ≥2 turns, show two buttons: **"Resume last session"** (loads turns into `st.session_state.messages`, keeps the existing `session_id`) and **"Start fresh"** (calls `new_session`). If no prior log, skip the prompt and start fresh silently.
- **Thread-cap for API calls** — when passing `messages` to `client.beta.messages.tool_runner`, cap at the last 40 turns. (Deferred enhancement: summarize older turns — out of scope for v2.2.)
- **Wire writes into the main loop** — in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py), call `append_turn` right after each user message and right after each assistant response. Keep `st.session_state.messages` as the in-memory mirror so the UI stays responsive.
- **"Start new session" button** in sidebar — clears `st.session_state.messages`, mints a fresh `session_id`, keeps the learner.

**1c. Identity on screen**
- **Header strip** — above `st.chat_input`, render `st.caption` with `👋 {name} · {level} · Today's focus: {focus_dimension}`. `focus_dimension` = lowest-scoring of the 5 assessment dimensions (deterministic, computed from `dimensionScores`). Labeled as "focus" (prescriptive) not "weakest area" (descriptive).
- **SAGE awareness of learner** — inject a small context block into the system prompt at runtime in [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py): `LEARNER_CONTEXT: name={name}, level={level}, dimensionScores={...}`. Lets skills address the learner by name and adapt vocabulary to their level without a tool round-trip.

**1d. Infrastructure**
- **Railway persistent volume** — configure via Railway dashboard: mount a volume at `/app/data`. On container boot, if the volume is empty, copy the seed profiles from the image into it. Document the volume setup in [README.md](README.md) under "Railway deployment."
- **CHANGELOG.md entry** — per project convention, append a dated entry describing Phase 1 additions.

### Phase 2 — Progress drawer

**Goal:** Learners who want detail can open the drawer and see where they stand and where they've been.

- **New helper module [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py)** with pure functions:
  - `render_level_badge(profile)` — styled label, used by header strip too.
  - `render_dimension_radar(profile)` — Plotly radar chart over the 5 dimensions (Streamlit has native Plotly support, no extra deps beyond what `pyproject.toml` already allows).
  - `render_competency_bars(profile)` — 4 horizontal bars, each with a one-sentence gloss next to the score (avoids the "high inference cost" OLM pitfall).
  - `render_reflection_log(profile)` — most recent 5 reflections from `profile["history"]`, collapsed further behind a "Show more" toggle.
- **Sidebar wiring in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — `with st.sidebar.expander("📊 My Progress", expanded=False):` then call the four render helpers. Drawer is hidden by default (research: ambient scores demotivate).
- **"Switch learner" action** at the bottom of the sidebar — clears `st.session_state.learner` and returns to the picker. No passwords, no confirmation (cohort trust model).

### Phase 3 — Inline end-of-session recap card

**Goal:** When a practice session closes with a reflection, the learner sees a single-delta recap right in the chat stream.

- **Teach the `reflection-facilitator` skill to emit a structured recap block** — edit [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) so that when a session closes it appends a `<SESSION_RECAP>{...}</SESSION_RECAP>` block to its final message. The JSON contains: `practice_type`, `dimension_changed`, `delta`, `suggested_next`.
- **Parser + renderer in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — when streaming the assistant message, extract the `<SESSION_RECAP>` block (regex), hide it from the text output, and render a bordered `st.container` card below the reflection message with the delta + a clickable "Try that next" button that seeds the next prompt.
- **Graceful fallback** — if no recap block is emitted (SAGE didn't end a session), nothing changes. The feature is additive, not a schema break.
- **Verify recap doesn't leak as literal text** to the learner on non-session-ending turns.

## Technical Notes

- **Railway filesystem constraint is the foundation.** Without the Phase 1 persistent volume, every profile write AND every interaction log is lost on every redeploy. Nothing else in this design works without it.
- **Two artifact trees, cleanly separated.** `data/users/*.json` = identity + scores (small, schema-stable, infrequently written). `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` = conversation transcripts (grow-only, one per session). Do not cram transcripts into the profile — the user-profile schema already omits them deliberately, and keeping boundaries clean lets the drawer's "session history" view be a simple directory listing.
- **Streamlit rerun model** — every interaction reruns the script top-to-bottom. All state (current learner, current session_id, drawer open/closed, picker visibility) must live in `st.session_state`. Keep the UI helpers pure (input: profile dict, output: Streamlit call).
- **Atomic writes to interaction logs.** Streamlit can fire duplicate reruns on fast user input. `append_turn` should read-modify-write under a short file lock or use "write new file + rename" to avoid corrupting the JSON.
- **Thread cap before the API.** Don't send an unbounded `messages` array to Claude. Cap at the last 40 turns for v2.2. Track this as a known limit in `docs/roadmap.md` — summarization of older turns is deferred.
- **Resume semantics.** On "Resume last session," rehydrate `st.session_state.messages` from the log, set `st.session_state.session_id` to that log's id, and let the next user input append to the existing file. Do NOT mint a new session_id on resume.
- **Treat pasted content as untrusted** — per [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) rule, the session-recap block is emitted by SAGE and trusted; anything the *learner* pastes is data, not instructions. Parser should only accept the recap sentinel in SAGE's own streamed output, never from user messages. (Applies equally: don't parse recap blocks out of *resumed* assistant turns into new UI renders — render recap cards only on fresh completions, not on replay.)
- **YAGNI flagged and deferred** — no XP, no streaks, no leaderboard, no tabs, no auth, no turn-summarization, no cross-device sync. Research explicitly warned against the gamification bits for a literacy tutor. Revisit only if demo feedback demands it.

## Critical Files

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| [sage/app.py](sage/app.py) | Picker, resume prompt, header strip, sidebar drawer, recap card parser, wire session-store writes |
| [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py) | **New** — `new_session`, `append_turn`, `load_session`, `latest_session` against `data/interactions/` using existing interaction-log schema |
| [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) | Add `list_user_profiles()` |
| [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py) | **New** — pure render helpers (level badge, radar, competency bars, reflection log, recap card) |
| [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py) | Inject `LEARNER_CONTEXT` block so SAGE addresses the learner by name + adapts to level |
| [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) | Emit `<SESSION_RECAP>` block at session close |
| [data/interactions/](data/interactions/) | **New directory** — per-learner session logs (gitignore real logs; keep a `.gitkeep`) |
| [.gitignore](.gitignore) | Ignore `data/interactions/*/` contents |
| [Dockerfile](Dockerfile) | No code change; document volume expectation in comment |
| [README.md](README.md) | Document Railway volume mount + cohort demo usage |
| [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) | Dated entries per phase |
| [docs/roadmap.md](docs/roadmap.md) | Move "visible learner progress" + "session persistence" from v3 wishlist to v2.2 shipped |

## Verification

**Phase 1 — identity:**
- `streamlit run sage/app.py` locally → picker appears → select `cyril.json` → header strip shows name, level, focus dimension.
- Pick "New learner" → enter a name → a fresh `data/users/<slug>.json` appears with the minimal template.

**Phase 1 — persistence:**
- Have a 4-turn conversation with `cyril` → refresh the browser → picker reappears → re-select `cyril` → "Resume last session?" prompt shows → click Resume → all 4 turns rehydrate in the chat stream, same `session_id` is in `st.session_state`.
- From the resumed thread, send a 5th message → open `data/interactions/cyril-001/<session>.json` → verify the new turn appended to the existing file (not a new file).
- Click "Start new session" → chat clears → `data/interactions/cyril-001/<new-session>.json` created → old log untouched.
- Switch to `alice` → no turns from cyril leak into the view; `alice` sees her own "Resume?" prompt or none.
- Deploy to Railway with volume mounted at `/app/data` → have a conversation → redeploy → resume the same session successfully.
- Deploy to Railway WITHOUT a volume → confirm data is lost (negative test proving the volume is the load-bearing piece).

**Phase 2:**
- Open the drawer → radar renders all 5 dimensions without errors on a new (all-zeros) profile.
- Competency bars each show gloss text.
- "Switch learner" returns to picker.

**Phase 3:**
- Run a full `scenario-runner` → `skill-evaluator` → `reflection-facilitator` sequence → recap card appears below the reflection question with a non-zero delta.
- Mid-conversation turn (no session ending) → no recap card leaks into chat.
- Inspect the raw streamed message → no literal `<SESSION_RECAP>` tag visible to the learner.

**Demo acceptance:**
- Two cohort members each pick their own profile, have a practice session, see their header + drawer + recap — and can refresh the browser without losing their profile state.

content.text:
Plan is coherent. Summary of the extension:

- **Phase 1 now has three sub-parts:** (1a) profile identity via picker, (1b) **conversation persistence via a new `sage/session_store.py`** that writes transcripts to `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` using the existing interaction-log schema, (1c) learner context injected into the system prompt so SAGE addresses them by name.
- **Resume flow:** after picking a learner, if a recent log exists with ≥2 turns, SAGE shows "Resume last session / Start fresh." Resume rehydrates the thread and keeps writing to the same file; fresh mints a new `session_id`.
- **Clean separation:** profile JSONs stay identity-only; transcripts live in their own tree. Profile schema already omits `turns` — that was intentional and we preserve the boundary.
- **Load-bearing piece:** Railway persistent volume at `/app/data`. Without it, none of this survives a redeploy.
- **Guardrails:** 40-turn cap before the API, atomic writes on append, resume doesn't re-render old recap cards.

input.plan:
# SAGE on Railway — Progress Visibility + Personalization

## Context

SAGE's Railway deploy was just unblocked (commit [08ae1f4](https://github.com/)) — it now runs as a Streamlit chat app on a public URL with Claude Opus 4.6 and 18 pedagogical skills. Pedagogy is complete; the UX surface is the weak spot:

- **No learner identity** — every visit is anonymous; profiles in `data/users/*.json` are never loaded. `sage/app.py` just initializes `st.session_state.messages = []` and never touches the profile tools.
- **No conversation continuity** — the chat thread lives only in `st.session_state.messages` (in-memory). A refresh, redeploy, or tab close wipes it. There's a `data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json` designed for session transcripts, but nothing writes to it.
- **No progress visibility** — the 5-dimension × 4-competency model SAGE tracks internally (schema already there) is invisible to the learner, so the "see yourself grow" pedagogy has no external surface.
- **Railway filesystem is ephemeral** — without a mounted volume, every write (profile or transcript) is lost on redeploy.

The cohort is about to demo SAGE. We want learners to feel **known** (their profile loads, their level is visible) and **tracked** (they can see what they've practiced and what's next) — without tripping the OLM/ed-tech pitfall of ambient dashboards that demotivate low performers (Bodily & Verbert 2018).

The research-backed UX shape (see brainstorm in this session): **always-visible minimal header + collapsible detail drawer + inline end-of-session recap card**. Borrowed from Khanmigo's session summary, Claude.ai's collapsible rail, Duolingo's jewel-level badge. Avoids tabs, XP, streaks, and ambient raw scores.

## Design Overview

Four user-facing additions to [sage/app.py](sage/app.py):

1. **Learner picker** on app start — no auth, trust-based, cohort-friendly. Dropdown of existing `data/users/*.json` profiles + "New learner" option that prompts for a name.
2. **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — if the picked learner has a recent session log, SAGE greets with "Welcome back. Last time we worked on output evaluation — want to pick up where you left off, or start fresh?"
3. **Header strip** (always visible, top of chat column) — `👋 cyril · Practitioner · Today's focus: Output Evaluation`. One line, no numbers. Prescriptive, not graded.
4. **"📊 My Progress" expander** in the sidebar — collapsed by default. Opens to 5-dimension radar + 4 competency bars + reflection log (read-only).
5. **Inline session-recap card** — renders as a chat-stream message after the closing reflection. "You practiced *Output Evaluation*. Ethical Reasoning +0.5. Next: try a prompt-crafting scenario?"

Plus two pieces of infrastructure:
- **Session store** — a new `sage/session_store.py` that persists the chat thread to `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` using the existing `interaction-log.schema.json`. Profile JSONs stay clean (identity + scores only); transcripts live in their own tree.
- **Railway persistent volume** mounted at `/app/data` so both `data/users/*.json` and `data/interactions/**` survive redeploys.

## Phased Rollout

### Phase 1 — Identity + conversation persistence (highest value, biggest surface)

**Goal:** Learners land on the app, pick themselves, and can pick up where they left off. SAGE "remembers" them across refreshes and redeploys.

**1a. Profile identity**
- **Add `list_user_profiles()` tool** in [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) — returns `[{"id": ..., "name": ..., "level": ..., "lastActiveAt": ...}]` by scanning `data/users/*.json`. Reuse the existing path-traversal guard pattern from `save_user_profile`.
- **Learner picker in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — on first load (no `st.session_state.learner`), render a centered `st.selectbox` of profiles + a "New learner" option (prompts for a name, then calls `save_user_profile` with a minimal template derived from the `novice` section of [user-profile.schema.json](data/schemas/user-profile.schema.json)).
- Selection loads the JSON into `st.session_state.learner` and unlocks the chat.

**1b. Conversation persistence (the "pick up where they left off" piece)**
- **New module [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py)** — single-responsibility module with four pure functions:
  - `new_session(user_id) -> session_id` — mints a UUID, creates `data/interactions/<user_id>/<session_id>.json` using the existing [interaction-log.schema.json](data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json).
  - `append_turn(user_id, session_id, role, content, metadata=None)` — atomic append to the log's `turns` array; updates `lastActiveAt` on the profile. Path-traversal guarded.
  - `load_session(user_id, session_id) -> list[turn]` — reads a log and returns messages in Anthropic-API-shape (`{"role": "user"|"assistant", "content": "..."}`).
  - `latest_session(user_id) -> session_id | None` — scans the learner's interactions dir, returns most recent by `timestamp` field, or `None` if no logs.
- **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — after picker, if `latest_session(user_id)` exists and has ≥2 turns, show two buttons: **"Resume last session"** (loads turns into `st.session_state.messages`, keeps the existing `session_id`) and **"Start fresh"** (calls `new_session`). If no prior log, skip the prompt and start fresh silently.
- **Thread-cap for API calls** — when passing `messages` to `client.beta.messages.tool_runner`, cap at the last 40 turns. (Deferred enhancement: summarize older turns — out of scope for v2.2.)
- **Wire writes into the main loop** — in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py), call `append_turn` right after each user message and right after each assistant response. Keep `st.session_state.messages` as the in-memory mirror so the UI stays responsive.
- **"Start new session" button** in sidebar — clears `st.session_state.messages`, mints a fresh `session_id`, keeps the learner.

### Phase 2 — Progress drawer

**Goal:** Learners who want detail can open the drawer and see where they stand and where they've been.

- **New helper module [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py)** with pure functions:

- **"Switch learner" action** at the bottom of the sidebar — clears `st.session_state.learner` and returns to the picker. No passwords, no confirmation (cohort trust model).

### Phase 3 — Inline end-of-session recap card

**Goal:** When a practice session closes with a reflection, the learner sees a single-delta recap right in the chat stream.

- **Teach the `reflection-facilitator` skill to emit a structured recap block** — edit [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) so that when a session closes it appends a `<SESSION_RECAP>{...}</SESSION_RECAP>` block to its final message. The JSON contains: `practice_type`, `dimension_changed`, `delta`, `suggested_next`.

- Inspect the raw streamed message → no literal `<SESSION_RECAP>` tag visible to the learner.

**Demo acceptance:**
- Two cohort members each pick their own profile, have a practice session, see their header + drawer + recap — and can refresh the browser without losing their profile state.

## Approved Plan (edited by user):
# SAGE on Railway — Progress Visibility + Personalization

## Context

SAGE's Railway deploy was just unblocked (commit [08ae1f4](https://github.com/)) — it now runs as a Streamlit chat app on a public URL with Claude Opus 4.6 and 18 pedagogical skills. Pedagogy is complete; the UX surface is the weak spot:

- **No learner identity** — every visit is anonymous; profiles in `data/users/*.json` are never loaded. `sage/app.py` just initializes `st.session_state.messages = []` and never touches the profile tools.
- **No conversation continuity** — the chat thread lives only in `st.session_state.messages` (in-memory). A refresh, redeploy, or tab close wipes it. There's a `data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json` designed for session transcripts, but nothing writes to it.
- **No progress visibility** — the 5-dimension × 4-competency model SAGE tracks internally (schema already there) is invisible to the learner, so the "see yourself grow" pedagogy has no external surface.
- **Railway filesystem is ephemeral** — without a mounted volume, every write (profile or transcript) is lost on redeploy.

The cohort is about to demo SAGE. We want learners to feel **known** (their profile loads, their level is visible) and **tracked** (they can see what they've practiced and what's next) — without tripping the OLM/ed-tech pitfall of ambient dashboards that demotivate low performers (Bodily & Verbert 2018).

The research-backed UX shape (see brainstorm in this session): **always-visible minimal header + collapsible detail drawer + inline end-of-session recap card**. Borrowed from Khanmigo's session summary, Claude.ai's collapsible rail, Duolingo's jewel-level badge. Avoids tabs, XP, streaks, and ambient raw scores.

## Design Overview

Four user-facing additions to [sage/app.py](sage/app.py):

1. **Learner picker** on app start — no auth, trust-based, cohort-friendly. Dropdown of existing `data/users/*.json` profiles + "New learner" option that prompts for a name.
2. **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — if the picked learner has a recent session log, SAGE greets with "Welcome back. Last time we worked on output evaluation — want to pick up where you left off, or start fresh?"
3. **Header strip** (always visible, top of chat column) — `👋 cyril · Practitioner · Today's focus: Output Evaluation`. One line, no numbers. Prescriptive, not graded.
4. **"📊 My Progress" expander** in the sidebar — collapsed by default. Opens to 5-dimension radar + 4 competency bars + reflection log (read-only).
5. **Inline session-recap card** — renders as a chat-stream message after the closing reflection. "You practiced *Output Evaluation*. Ethical Reasoning +0.5. Next: try a prompt-crafting scenario?"

Plus two pieces of infrastructure:
- **Session store** — a new `sage/session_store.py` that persists the chat thread to `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` using the existing `interaction-log.schema.json`. Profile JSONs stay clean (identity + scores only); transcripts live in their own tree.
- **Railway persistent volume** mounted at `/app/data` so both `data/users/*.json` and `data/interactions/**` survive redeploys.

## Phased Rollout

### Phase 1 — Identity + conversation persistence (highest value, biggest surface)

**Goal:** Learners land on the app, pick themselves, and can pick up where they left off. SAGE "remembers" them across refreshes and redeploys.

**1a. Profile identity**
- **Add `list_user_profiles()` tool** in [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) — returns `[{"id": ..., "name": ..., "level": ..., "lastActiveAt": ...}]` by scanning `data/users/*.json`. Reuse the existing path-traversal guard pattern from `save_user_profile`.
- **Learner picker in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — on first load (no `st.session_state.learner`), render a centered `st.selectbox` of profiles + a "New learner" option (prompts for a name, then calls `save_user_profile` with a minimal template derived from the `novice` section of [user-profile.schema.json](data/schemas/user-profile.schema.json)).
- Selection loads the JSON into `st.session_state.learner` and unlocks the chat.

**1b. Conversation persistence (the "pick up where they left off" piece)**
- **New module [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py)** — single-responsibility module with four pure functions:
  - `new_session(user_id) -> session_id` — mints a UUID, creates `data/interactions/<user_id>/<session_id>.json` using the existing [interaction-log.schema.json](data/schemas/interaction-log.schema.json).
  - `append_turn(user_id, session_id, role, content, metadata=None)` — atomic append to the log's `turns` array; updates `lastActiveAt` on the profile. Path-traversal guarded.
  - `load_session(user_id, session_id) -> list[turn]` — reads a log and returns messages in Anthropic-API-shape (`{"role": "user"|"assistant", "content": "..."}`).
  - `latest_session(user_id) -> session_id | None` — scans the learner's interactions dir, returns most recent by `timestamp` field, or `None` if no logs.
- **Resume-or-fresh prompt** — after picker, if `latest_session(user_id)` exists and has ≥2 turns, show two buttons: **"Resume last session"** (loads turns into `st.session_state.messages`, keeps the existing `session_id`) and **"Start fresh"** (calls `new_session`). If no prior log, skip the prompt and start fresh silently.
- **Thread-cap for API calls** — when passing `messages` to `client.beta.messages.tool_runner`, cap at the last 40 turns. (Deferred enhancement: summarize older turns — out of scope for v2.2.)
- **Wire writes into the main loop** — in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py), call `append_turn` right after each user message and right after each assistant response. Keep `st.session_state.messages` as the in-memory mirror so the UI stays responsive.
- **"Start new session" button** in sidebar — clears `st.session_state.messages`, mints a fresh `session_id`, keeps the learner.

**1c. Identity on screen**
- **Header strip** — above `st.chat_input`, render `st.caption` with `👋 {name} · {level} · Today's focus: {focus_dimension}`. `focus_dimension` = lowest-scoring of the 5 assessment dimensions (deterministic, computed from `dimensionScores`). Labeled as "focus" (prescriptive) not "weakest area" (descriptive).
- **SAGE awareness of learner** — inject a small context block into the system prompt at runtime in [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py): `LEARNER_CONTEXT: name={name}, level={level}, dimensionScores={...}`. Lets skills address the learner by name and adapt vocabulary to their level without a tool round-trip.

**1d. Infrastructure**
- **Railway persistent volume** — configure via Railway dashboard: mount a volume at `/app/data`. On container boot, if the volume is empty, copy the seed profiles from the image into it. Document the volume setup in [README.md](README.md) under "Railway deployment."
- **CHANGELOG.md entry** — per project convention, append a dated entry describing Phase 1 additions.

### Phase 2 — Progress drawer

**Goal:** Learners who want detail can open the drawer and see where they stand and where they've been.

- **New helper module [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py)** with pure functions:
  - `render_level_badge(profile)` — styled label, used by header strip too.
  - `render_dimension_radar(profile)` — Plotly radar chart over the 5 dimensions (Streamlit has native Plotly support, no extra deps beyond what `pyproject.toml` already allows).
  - `render_competency_bars(profile)` — 4 horizontal bars, each with a one-sentence gloss next to the score (avoids the "high inference cost" OLM pitfall).
  - `render_reflection_log(profile)` — most recent 5 reflections from `profile["history"]`, collapsed further behind a "Show more" toggle.
- **Sidebar wiring in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — `with st.sidebar.expander("📊 My Progress", expanded=False):` then call the four render helpers. Drawer is hidden by default (research: ambient scores demotivate).
- **"Switch learner" action** at the bottom of the sidebar — clears `st.session_state.learner` and returns to the picker. No passwords, no confirmation (cohort trust model).

### Phase 3 — Inline end-of-session recap card

**Goal:** When a practice session closes with a reflection, the learner sees a single-delta recap right in the chat stream.

- **Teach the `reflection-facilitator` skill to emit a structured recap block** — edit [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) so that when a session closes it appends a `<SESSION_RECAP>{...}</SESSION_RECAP>` block to its final message. The JSON contains: `practice_type`, `dimension_changed`, `delta`, `suggested_next`.
- **Parser + renderer in [sage/app.py](sage/app.py)** — when streaming the assistant message, extract the `<SESSION_RECAP>` block (regex), hide it from the text output, and render a bordered `st.container` card below the reflection message with the delta + a clickable "Try that next" button that seeds the next prompt.
- **Graceful fallback** — if no recap block is emitted (SAGE didn't end a session), nothing changes. The feature is additive, not a schema break.
- **Verify recap doesn't leak as literal text** to the learner on non-session-ending turns.

## Technical Notes

- **Railway filesystem constraint is the foundation.** Without the Phase 1 persistent volume, every profile write AND every interaction log is lost on every redeploy. Nothing else in this design works without it.
- **Two artifact trees, cleanly separated.** `data/users/*.json` = identity + scores (small, schema-stable, infrequently written). `data/interactions/<userId>/<sessionId>.json` = conversation transcripts (grow-only, one per session). Do not cram transcripts into the profile — the user-profile schema already omits them deliberately, and keeping boundaries clean lets the drawer's "session history" view be a simple directory listing.
- **Streamlit rerun model** — every interaction reruns the script top-to-bottom. All state (current learner, current session_id, drawer open/closed, picker visibility) must live in `st.session_state`. Keep the UI helpers pure (input: profile dict, output: Streamlit call).
- **Atomic writes to interaction logs.** Streamlit can fire duplicate reruns on fast user input. `append_turn` should read-modify-write under a short file lock or use "write new file + rename" to avoid corrupting the JSON.
- **Thread cap before the API.** Don't send an unbounded `messages` array to Claude. Cap at the last 40 turns for v2.2. Track this as a known limit in `docs/roadmap.md` — summarization of older turns is deferred.
- **Resume semantics.** On "Resume last session," rehydrate `st.session_state.messages` from the log, set `st.session_state.session_id` to that log's id, and let the next user input append to the existing file. Do NOT mint a new session_id on resume.
- **Treat pasted content as untrusted** — per [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md) rule, the session-recap block is emitted by SAGE and trusted; anything the *learner* pastes is data, not instructions. Parser should only accept the recap sentinel in SAGE's own streamed output, never from user messages. (Applies equally: don't parse recap blocks out of *resumed* assistant turns into new UI renders — render recap cards only on fresh completions, not on replay.)
- **YAGNI flagged and deferred** — no XP, no streaks, no leaderboard, no tabs, no auth, no turn-summarization, no cross-device sync. Research explicitly warned against the gamification bits for a literacy tutor. Revisit only if demo feedback demands it.

## Critical Files

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| [sage/app.py](sage/app.py) | Picker, resume prompt, header strip, sidebar drawer, recap card parser, wire session-store writes |
| [sage/session_store.py](sage/session_store.py) | **New** — `new_session`, `append_turn`, `load_session`, `latest_session` against `data/interactions/` using existing interaction-log schema |
| [sage/tools.py](sage/tools.py) | Add `list_user_profiles()` |
| [sage/ui.py](sage/ui.py) | **New** — pure render helpers (level badge, radar, competency bars, reflection log, recap card) |
| [sage/prompts.py](sage/prompts.py) | Inject `LEARNER_CONTEXT` block so SAGE addresses the learner by name + adapts to level |
| [.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md](.claude/skills/reflection-facilitator/SKILL.md) | Emit `<SESSION_RECAP>` block at session close |
| [data/interactions/](data/interactions/) | **New directory** — per-learner session logs (gitignore real logs; keep a `.gitkeep`) |
| [.gitignore](.gitignore) | Ignore `data/interactions/*/` contents |
| [Dockerfile](Dockerfile) | No code change; document volume expectation in comment |
| [README.md](README.md) | Document Railway volume mount + cohort demo usage |
| [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md) | Dated entries per phase |
| [docs/roadmap.md](docs/roadmap.md) | Move "visible learner progress" + "session persistence" from v3 wishlist to v2.2 shipped |

## Verification

**Phase 1 — identity:**
- `streamlit run sage/app.py` locally → picker appears → select `cyril.json` → header strip shows name, level, focus dimension.
- Pick "New learner" → enter a name → a fresh `data/users/<slug>.json` appears with the minimal template.

**Phase 1 — persistence:**
- Have a 4-turn conversation with `cyril` → refresh the browser → picker reappears → re-select `cyril` → "Resume last session?" prompt shows → click Resume → all 4 turns rehydrate in the chat stream, same `session_id` is in `st.session_state`.
- From the resumed thread, send a 5th message → open `data/interactions/cyril-001/<session>.json` → verify the new turn appended to the existing file (not a new file).
- Click "Start new session" → chat clears → `data/interactions/cyril-001/<new-session>.json` created → old log untouched.
- Switch to `alice` → no turns from cyril leak into the view; `alice` sees her own "Resume?" prompt or none.
- Deploy to Railway with volume mounted at `/app/data` → have a conversation → redeploy → resume the same session successfully.
- Deploy to Railway WITHOUT a volume → confirm data is lost (negative test proving the volume is the load-bearing piece).

**Phase 2:**
- Open the drawer → radar renders all 5 dimensions without errors on a new (all-zeros) profile.
- Competency bars each show gloss text.
- "Switch learner" returns to picker.

**Phase 3:**
- Run a full `scenario-runner` → `skill-evaluator` → `reflection-facilitator` sequence → recap card appears below the reflection question with a non-zero delta.
- Mid-conversation turn (no session ending) → no recap card leaks into chat.
- Inspect the raw streamed message → no literal `<SESSION_RECAP>` tag visible to the learner.

**Demo acceptance:**
- Two cohort members each pick their own profile, have a practice session, see their header + drawer + recap — and can refresh the browser without losing their profile state.

| [docs/roadmap.md](docs/roadmap.md) | Move "visible learner progress" + "session persistence" from v3 wishlist to v2.2 shipped |

## Verification

**Phase 1 — identity:**
- `streamlit run sage/app.py` locally → picker appears → select `cyril.json` → header strip shows name, level, focus dimension.
- Pick "New learner" → enter a name → a fresh `data/users/<slug>.json` appears with the minimal template.

**Phase 1 — persistence:**
- Have a 4-turn conversation with `cyril` → refresh the browser → picker reappears → re-select `cyril` → "Resume last session?" prompt shows → click Resume → all 4 turns rehydrate in the chat stream, same `session_id` is in `st.session_state`.
- From the resumed thread, send a 5th message → open `data/interactions/cyril-001/<session>.json` → verify the new turn appended to the existing file (not a new file).
- Click "Start new session" → chat clears → `data/interactions/cyril-001/<new-session>.json` created → old log untouched.
- Switch to `alice` → no turns from cyril leak into the view; `alice` sees her own "Resume?" prompt or none.
- Deploy to Railway with volume mounted at `/app/data` → have a conversation → redeploy → resume the same session successfully.
- Deploy to Railway WITHOUT a volume → confirm data is lost (negative test proving the volume is the load-bearing piece).

**Phase 2:**
- Open the drawer → radar renders all 5 dimensions without errors on a new (all-zeros) profile.
- Competency bars each show gloss text.
- "Switch learner" returns to picker.

**Phase 3:**
- Run a full `scenario-runner` → `skill-evaluator` → `reflection-facilitator` sequence → recap card appears below the reflection question with a non-zero delta.
- Mid-conversation turn (no session ending) → no recap card leaks into chat.
- Inspect the raw streamed message → no literal `<SESSION_RECAP>` tag visible to the learner.

**Demo acceptance:**
- Two cohort members each pick their own profile, have a practice session, see their header + drawer + recap — and can refresh the browser without losing their profile state.
